creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
One of these Thailand travel mistakes can literally send you to jail 😅

Thailand is one of my all time favourite travel destinations, but there are a few things we Westernes should know before visiting the stunning South East Asian country 🫡

Do you have any other “Thailand dont’s” you wanna share? 👀

#thailandtravel #traveltips #thailandtrip

transcription.text: Avoid making these common tourist mistakes when you're coming to Thailand. Don't assume the bathrooms have got toilet paper. So make sure you always carry tissues, because literally no public toilets in Thailand have toilet paper. Don't assume tuk-tuks in Bangkok are cheaper than taxis. If you want to ride a tuk-tuk, then it should be for the experience, as taxis are way cheaper and more safe. But if you want to ride a tuk-tuk, then make sure that you negotiate the price before getting on, so you don't get scammed. Don't talk about the royal family in a negative way. This could literally send you straight to jail. Don't ride an elephant. If you're thinking about riding an elephant, please do a bit of research, and if you still want to do it after finding out about how insanely cruel it is, then you truly are a scumbag. Don't walk around with your top off anywhere else than on the beach. It's seen as incredibly tacky and as a lack of respect, so just put on that Changtang top, bro. Don't ride a scooter without a helmet. Just because you're on your holiday doesn't mean you're immortal, and last year Thailand was ranked as the world's second most dangerous country to drive in.
